item.title: Trapping an electron in a box
item.description: The Action Lab explores a physics experiment about trapping an electron in a box and what it can reveal about light and science.

media:content.media:title: Trapping an electron in a box
media:content.media:description: The Action Lab explores a physics experiment about trapping an electron in a box and what it can reveal about light and science.

item.title: Swimming in Syrup vs. Water
item.description: In this video I test out the proposition that swimming in syrup is as easy as swimming in water. Based on a research paper published at the University of Minnesota

media:content.media:title: Swimming in Syrup vs. Water
media:content.media:description: In this video I test out the proposition that swimming in syrup is as easy as swimming in water. Based on a research paper published at the University of Minnesota

item.title: Is negative mass possible?
item.description: In this video I show you a real experiment that shows how something with a negative mass reacts to a force applied to it. Then I talk about negative mass and if it really is a physical impossibility.

media:content.media:title: Is negative mass possible?
media:content.media:description: In this video I show you a real experiment that shows how something with a negative mass reacts to a force applied to it. Then I talk about negative mass and if it really is a physical impossibility.

item.title: The Kamifusen Paper Balloon Paradox
item.description: In this video I show you on odd phenomenon in which a paper balloon inflates itself when it seems it should be deflating. How does the Japanese paper ball, the Kamifusen, work? How does it inflate itself?

media:content.media:title: The Kamifusen Paper Balloon Paradox
media:content.media:description: In this video I show you on odd phenomenon in which a paper balloon inflates itself when it seems it should be deflating. How does the Japanese paper ball, the Kamifusen, work? How does it inflate itself?

item.title: How much air is in ice cream?
item.description: In this video I put Ice cream in my vacuum chamber! I talk about the rules of Ice cream (yes, they exist)-how much air they can have and how much fat they must have to be called ice cream.

media:content.media:title: How much air is in ice cream?
media:content.media:description: In this video I put Ice cream in my vacuum chamber! I talk about the rules of Ice cream (yes, they exist)-how much air they can have and how much fat they must have to be called ice cream.

item.title: Can you lift yourself up?
item.description: In this video I do an experiment to see if it is possible to pick yourself up. If it isn't possible I show you the actual forces behind it by using a scale while I'm trying to pick myself up. I show you the closest thing to being able to pick yourself up off the ground without jumping. Then I talk about momentum and finally negative mass as a source of anti-gravity which bends space time the opposite direction.

media:content.media:title: Can you lift yourself up?
media:content.media:description: In this video I do an experiment to see if it is possible to pick yourself up. If it isn't possible I show you the actual forces behind it by using a scale while I'm trying to pick myself up. I show you the closest thing to being able to pick yourself up off the ground without jumping. Then I talk about momentum and finally negative mass as a source of anti-gravity which bends space time the opposite direction.

item.title: Acoustic rockets with Helmholtz resonance
item.description: Acoustic rockets and how Helmholtz resonance works

media:content.media:title: Acoustic rockets with Helmholtz resonance
media:content.media:description: Acoustic rockets and how Helmholtz resonance works

item.title: Quantum physics just got cooler
item.description: Science experiment exploring quantum physics with magnets.

media:content.media:title: Quantum physics just got cooler
media:content.media:description: Science experiment exploring quantum physics with magnets.

item.title: The ultimate lens flare
item.description: The Action Lab explores lens flare in a science experiment focused on light and physics.

media:content.media:title: The ultimate lens flare
media:content.media:description: The Action Lab explores lens flare in a science experiment focused on light and physics.

item.title: Can Pop Rocks carbonate soda?
item.description: In this video I see if it is possible to carbonate soda by adding 22 bags of pop rocks in it. First, in order to get my soda to have no carbonation and be completely flat, I put it in the vacuum chamber. Then I add the pop rocks to pressurize the soda with CO2 and see if it actually adds carbonation to the soda.

media:content.media:title: Can Pop Rocks carbonate soda?
media:content.media:description: In this video I see if it is possible to carbonate soda by adding 22 bags of pop rocks in it. First, in order to get my soda to have no carbonation and be completely flat, I put it in the vacuum chamber. Then I add the pop rocks to pressurize the soda with CO2 and see if it actually adds carbonation to the soda.

item.title: Does a syringe work in a vacuum?
item.description: Exploring how a syringe behaves in a vacuum

media:content.media:title: Does a syringe work in a vacuum?
media:content.media:description: Exploring how a syringe behaves in a vacuum
